name: WorldView (Maxar legacy)

overview.value: Vantor's satellite constellation page lists WorldView Legion, WorldView-3, WorldView-2, GeoEye-1 and WorldView-1 as part of its imaging satellite fleet. Vantor describes its constellation of 10 imaging satellites as able to revisit the same location on Earth 15 times per day, with downlink speeds as fast as 15 minutes after collection.

base.reason: SpaceNews (trade press), reporting Vantor's April 8, 2026 statement: 'Vantor's existing fleet, composed of six WorldView Legion satellites and four legacy spacecraft'; corroborated by Vantor's own constellation page naming exactly WorldView-1, WorldView-2, WorldView-3 and GeoEye-1 as non-Legion fleet members, and by this profile's sats_active_verified (CelesTrak, value 4)

notes: Maxar's legacy WorldView satellites; WorldView Legion has its own profile. Maxar Intelligence rebranded to Vantor. Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); sats_active_claimed filled 2026-07-09. sats_launched_total and status stay null: no single page states a combined cumulative-launched figure or fleet-wide status word for this WorldView-plus-GeoEye-1 grouping (WorldView-4 retired 2019, reentered 2021, per Wikipedia/Space.com) without summing across sources, which this site's rules disallow.
